hero.badge: Indonesia — Bali · Yogyakarta · Bromo · Lake Toba
hero.headline: 17,000 Islands.
hero.body: The world's largest archipelago stretches 5,000 kilometres across the equator — more islands than you could visit in a lifetime. But certain ones keep drawing people back. Bali for its temples, terraces and beach sunsets. Yogyakarta for Borobudur, the largest Buddhist monument on Earth. Mount Bromo for a 4WD sunrise across a sea of volcanic ash. Lake Toba for the largest volcanic lake in the world. Lombok for the reef and the mountain and the quiet. These are the ones. We'll take you there.

why.heading: The Destinations That Define Indonesia
why.subheading: From the Hindu temple island of Bali to the Buddhist monuments of Yogyakarta, the volcanic landscapes of Java and the crater lakes of Sumatra — Indonesia rewards every type of traveller.

highlights.title: Bali — Island of the Gods
highlights.body: Bali is not one thing. It is Uluwatu's clifftop temple above the Indian Ocean, Tegalalang's UNESCO rice terraces carved into volcanic hillsides, Ubud's traditional market and Royal Palace, Tanah Lot perched on a sea rock at sunset, and Nusa Penida's broken beach and manta rays. The island that appears on every bucket list earns it — but the real Bali is the one you find between the famous sights: the offerings at the temple gate every morning, the gamelan music drifting from a compound, the roadside warung.

highlights.title: Borobudur — Largest Buddhist Monument on Earth
highlights.body: Built in the 9th century during the Sailendra dynasty and abandoned for nearly a thousand years until Dutch colonial archaeologists began restoration in 1907, Borobudur is 2 million stone blocks assembled into a mandala you walk around and through. The 504 Buddha statues, 2,672 relief panels depicting the life of the Buddha and Javanese court life, and 72 stupas containing meditating Buddhas make it simultaneously a religious monument, an art history lesson, and one of the great human achievements in stone. A sunrise visit — arriving before the tour groups at 06:00 — remains one of the most affecting experiences in Southeast Asia.

highlights.title: Mount Bromo — Active Volcano at Dawn
highlights.body: The 2:00 AM wake-up call is non-negotiable. You board a 4WD jeep in darkness, drive up the caldera rim to the Penanjakan viewpoint, and wait. As the sun rises over the volcanic peaks, Mount Bromo's smoking crater emerges from the Sea of Sand — a flat expanse of volcanic ash 10 kilometres across — with steam rising from the active vent 200 metres below. The jeep ride back down through the Sea of Sand to the crater rim, then the 253-step climb to look into an active Indonesian volcano: there is no experience in Malaysia that compares.

highlights.title: Lake Toba — World's Largest Volcanic Lake
highlights.body: Lake Toba was formed by the largest volcanic eruption in the last 2 million years — an event so catastrophic it may have reduced the human population to fewer than 10,000 individuals. Today the caldera holds a lake 100 kilometres long and 30 kilometres wide, with Samosir Island — larger than Singapore — floating in its centre. Samosir is the homeland of the Batak people, and their traditional villages, tombs of ancient kings, and ceremonial architecture survive here as a living culture, not a museum reconstruction.

zones.heading: 7 Destinations, One Archipelago
zones.subheading: Bali for temples and beaches. Yogyakarta for UNESCO monuments. Java's volcanoes for the most dramatic sunrises in Asia. Sumatra for ancient crater lakes and colonial hill towns. Lombok for reef and mountain.

areas.name: Bali
areas.description: 13 packages from RM398. Uluwatu sunset, Ubud rice terraces, Tanah Lot, Nusa Penida cliffs and Tegalalang — the island that defines Indonesian tourism delivers. Private tours, full-day programmes, island hopping and romantic escapes.

areas.name: Yogyakarta
areas.description: 5 packages from RM988. Borobudur at sunrise, Prambanan's Hindu temple complex, Mount Merapi 4WD jeep tour, Taman Sari Water Castle and the Sultan's Kraton palace. The cultural capital of Java.

areas.name: Medan / Lake Toba
areas.description: 2 packages from RM788. Lake Toba — the world's largest volcanic lake — with Samosir Island, Batak tribal villages, ancient royal tombs, Sipiso-Piso waterfall and Berastagi highland market.

areas.name: Lombok
areas.description: 2 packages from RM688. Mount Rinjani's crater lake, the Gili islands, Senggigi beach, Kuta Lombok surf breaks and traditional Sasak villages. Bali's quieter, wilder neighbour.

areas.name: Padang / Bukittinggi
areas.description: 1 package from RM988. Harau Valley's 150-metre granite cliffs, Bukittinggi's Dutch fort and clock tower, Sianok Canyon gorge, Ngarai viewpoint and Minangkabau traditional architecture — West Sumatra's highland circuit.

areas.name: Surabaya / Bromo
areas.description: 2 packages from RM1,388. Mount Bromo 4WD sunrise, Museum Angkut Asia's largest transport museum, Coban Rondo waterfall, Malang's colourful Jodipan village and Surabaya's historic Old Batavia quarter.

areas.name: Jakarta / Bandung
areas.description: 2 packages from RM1,288. Kawah Putih White Crater turquoise lake, Bandung factory outlets and highlands, National Monument (Monas), WHOOSH bullet train, Taman Mini Indonesia Indah and Old Batavia waterfront.

bestTime.heading: Best Time to Visit Indonesia from Malaysia
bestTime.body: Indonesia straddles the equator and most destinations have a wet and dry season. The dry season is generally the safest for outdoor activities, though Bali and Yogyakarta are visited year-round.

seasons.name: Dry Season
seasons.description: Ideal for Bali beach and temple visits, Bromo sunrise, Yogyakarta temples, and Lombok island hopping. Clear skies and calm seas. July and August are peak season — book early. Lake Toba and Padang highlands are pleasant year-round.

seasons.name: Shoulder & Wet Season
seasons.description: November–March brings afternoon rain across Java and Bali, though mornings are usually clear for sightseeing. Hotel and package rates drop 20–30%. Bromo sunrise is still accessible but may require more flexible scheduling. Sumatra (Lake Toba, Padang) is less affected by Java's monsoon.

options.label: One Destination
options.description: Ideal for Bali (3D/2N covers Uluwatu, Ubud and Tanah Lot) or Yogyakarta (Borobudur, Merapi, Malioboro). A long weekend from Kuala Lumpur.

options.label: Best Value
options.description: Adds an island day in Bali (Nusa Penida or island hopping) or the full Yogyakarta–Prambanan circuit. The sweet spot for most travellers.

options.label: Full Experience
options.description: Combine two destinations — Yogyakarta and Bromo/Malang, or a 5-day Bali with highlands and sunset cruise. The most complete view of one island or region.

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Indonesia Trip Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Indonesia's scale is the challenge. From Kuala Lumpur there are direct flights to Bali, Yogyakarta, Lombok, Medan, Surabaya and Jakarta — but each island and city has its own seasons, its own operators, its own unwritten rules about what to do first and what to skip. Dhesu has been arranging Indonesia travel for Malaysian clients for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.description: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. All Indonesia packages are arranged through licensed local operators in each city. Your money is protected.

points.title: Trusted Since 1988
points.description: Over 35 years of sending Malaysian travellers to Indonesia. We know the Bromo 2AM wake-up drill, the Borobudur sunrise permit system, and when to go to Bali and when to avoid.

points.title: 100% Private Tours
points.description: Every Indonesia package from Dhesu is completely private — your own driver-guide, your own vehicle, your own schedule. No waiting for strangers, no group dynamics.

points.title: Group Discounts Available
points.description: Significant reductions for groups of 3 and 5 adults. Multi-destination custom itineraries combining two or more Indonesian cities are available on request.
